entries.description: Linux CPU usage from /proc/stat (instant, no sampling delay)
Linux CPU usage from top (fallback, requires sampling)
macOS CPU usage from top
Windows CPU usage from WMI CIM

parser.script: local total, avail = 0, 0 for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do local key, val = line:match('(%w+):%s+(%d+)') if key == 'MemTotal' then total = tonumber(val) elseif key == 'MemAvailable' then avail = tonumber(val) end end if total == 0 then return 0 end return (total - avail) / total * 100

entries.description: Linux RAM usage percentage from /proc/meminfo
Linux total RAM in MB from /proc/meminfo

parser.script: local total, avail = 0, 0 for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do local key, val = line:match('(%w+):%s+(%d+)') if key == 'MemTotal' then total = tonumber(val) elseif key == 'MemAvailable' then avail = tonumber(val) end end return (total - avail) / 1024

entries.description: Linux used RAM in MB from /proc/meminfo

parser.script: local ps = 16384 local free, active, inactive, spec, wired = 0,0,0,0,0 for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do if line:match('page size of') then ps = tonumber(line:match('(%d+) bytes')) or ps end local v = tonumber(line:match(':%s+(%d+)')) if line:match('Pages free') then free = v or 0 elseif line:match('Pages active') then active = v or 0 elseif line:match('Pages inactive') then inactive = v or 0 elseif line:match('Pages speculative') then spec = v or 0 elseif line:match('Pages wired') then wired = v or 0 end end local total = (free+active+inactive+spec+wired)*ps/1048576 local used = (active+wired)*ps/1048576 if total == 0 then return 0 end return used/total*100

entries.description: macOS RAM usage from vm_stat

parser.script: local parts = {} for w in output:gmatch('%S+') do parts[#parts+1] = tonumber(w) end if #parts < 2 or parts[1] == 0 then return 0 end return (parts[1] - parts[2]) / parts[1] * 100

entries.description: Windows RAM usage from WMI CIM
Windows total RAM in MB from WMI

parser.script: local parts = {} for w in output:gmatch('%S+') do parts[#parts+1] = tonumber(w) end if #parts < 2 then return 0 end return (parts[1] - parts[2]) / 1024

entries.description: Windows used RAM in MB from WMI
Linux free disk space in GB from df
macOS free disk space in GB from df
Windows free disk space in GB from WMI
NVIDIA GPU utilization via nvidia-smi

parser.script: local parts = {} for w in output:gmatch('[^,]+') do parts[#parts+1] = tonumber(w:match('%d+')) end if #parts < 2 or parts[2] == 0 then return 0 end return parts[1] / parts[2] * 100

entries.description: NVIDIA VRAM usage percentage via nvidia-smi
NVIDIA VRAM used in MB
NVIDIA VRAM total in MB
NVIDIA GPU temperature in Celsius

entries.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "Add-Type -TypeDefinition 'using System; using System.Runtime.InteropServices; public struct LASTINPUTINFO { public uint cbSize; public uint dwTime; } public class IdleCheck { [DllImport(\"user32.dll\")] public static extern bool GetLastInputInfo(ref LASTINPUTINFO lii); public static uint GetIdleSecs() { var lii = new LASTINPUTINFO(); lii.cbSize = (uint)Marshal.SizeOf(lii); GetLastInputInfo(ref lii); return (uint)(Environment.TickCount - lii.dwTime) / 1000; } }'; [IdleCheck]::GetIdleSecs()"
entries.description: Windows idle time via GetLastInputInfo P/Invoke

parser.script: local ns = tonumber(output:match('(%d+)')) if ns then return ns / 1000000000 else return 0 end

entries.description: macOS idle time from IOKit HIDIdleTime (nanoseconds to seconds)
Linux idle time via xprintidle (ms to seconds)
Linux system uptime as idle proxy (headless servers)

parser.script: local mtime = tonumber(output:match('(%d+)')) if mtime then return os.time() - mtime else return 0 end

entries.description: Linux idle time from input device modification time
Unix logged-in users from who
macOS logged-in users from who

parser.script: local users = {} local first = true for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do if first then first = false else local user = line:match('^>?(%S+)') if user then users[#users+1] = user end end end return table.concat(users, '\n')

entries.description: Windows logged-in users from query user

parser.script: local procs = {} for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do local parts = {} for w in line:gmatch('%S+') do parts[#parts+1] = w end if #parts >= 11 then local pid = parts[2] local cpu = parts[3] local mem = tonumber(parts[6]) or 0 local name = table.concat(parts, ' ', 11) procs[#procs+1] = pid..' '..cpu..' '..string.format('%.1f', mem/1024)..' '..name end end table.sort(procs, function(a,b) local ac = tonumber(a:match('^%S+ (%S+)')) or 0 local bc = tonumber(b:match('^%S+ (%S+)')) or 0 return ac > bc end) local result = {} for i=1, math.min(20, #procs) do result[#result+1] = procs[i] end return table.concat(result, '\n')

entries.description: Linux process list from ps aux

parser.script: local procs = {} local first = true for line in output:gmatch('[^\n]+') do if first then first = false else local parts = {} for w in line:gmatch('%S+') do parts[#parts+1] = w end if #parts >= 11 then local pid = parts[2] local cpu = parts[3] local mem = tonumber(parts[6]) or 0 local name = table.concat(parts, ' ', 11) procs[#procs+1] = pid..' '..cpu..' '..string.format('%.1f', mem/1024)..' '..name end end end local result = {} for i=1, math.min(20, #procs) do result[#result+1] = procs[i] end return table.concat(result, '\n')

entries.description: macOS process list from ps aux -r
Windows process list from Get-Process
